site_tagline: Get a shareable link to download a build artifact from the latest successful GitHub Actions build.
public_note: Any public repository is accessible by default and visitors don't need to log in.
optional_auth: (optional, except for private repos; you can revoke anytime)
paste_prompt: Paste a GitHub link, get a dawnl.ink!
check_sections: Check out the sections below to see what you can do.
check_formats: Please check below for recognized link formats:
link_not_recognized: Did not detect a link to a GitHub workflow file, actions run, or artifact.
section_latest: Link to a repository's latest artifact
section_latest_desc: Insert the GitHub URL of a workflow file that uses actions/upload-artifact.
branch_matters: Note that the branch you are on also matters.
section_artifact_intro: If GitHub gave you a link such as the one below, change the github.com prefix to this site:
section_artifact_outro: You get a download URL that works the same but does not show a 404 to visitors who are not logged into GitHub.
section_artifact_paste: Or paste the GitHub link into the field above.
section_run: Extra links for a particular run
section_run_intro: A run is basically a collection of jobs. Even though it is the job that produces artifacts, they are associated with the parent run.
section_run_must: You must provide the run to dawnl.ink to find artifacts. Again, change the prefix like in the example above.
section_run_job_intro: If you click into a job instead, you can access its logs. Example job:
section_run_job_outro: Raw log downloads are in the same situation as artifacts: dawnl.ink is needed for anonymous users. It accepts the job URL and the link from the "View raw logs" menu item.
section_bot: Bot that comments on pull requests
section_bot_p1: For projects developing an application, it is useful to quickly try out the executable built from an incoming pull request. You can do this by clicking into a finished GitHub Actions job, then its parent run, then scrolling to the artifacts list.
section_bot_p2: Instead, you can use a more convenient approach: add a bot that replies with links directly to the artifacts as soon as the pull request finishes building.
section_bot_p3: Apply it to your repository by copying
section_bot_p3_note: It must be on the default branch and fully merged before it will do anything.
section_bot_p4: Another advantage is that users who are not logged into GitHub can download the artifacts. If you do not need that, adapt the sample workflow to link directly to github.com without this site.
section_bot_p5: This ability is not part of dawnl.ink itself, just a creative way to apply it; the bot runs entirely within GitHub Actions in your repository.
dashboard_desc: Repositories accessible through your GitHub App installation:
no_installations: No installations found. Install the GitHub App first.
repo_workflows_subtitle: Choose a workflow to generate dawnl.ink links
repo_workflows_empty: No active GitHub Actions workflows found for this repository.
repo_workflows_badge_preview: Latest build status (links to dawnl.ink artifacts)
repo_workflows_badge_hint: Paste the Markdown into your README. The badge reflects the latest run; the link opens dawnl.ink artifacts for this workflow on the default branch.
artifact_access: You can access this artifact by one of the following links, from least to most direct:
ephemeral_link: Ephemeral direct download link (expires in <1 minute)
stable_workflow_link: Permanent download link (latest successful build on this branch)
ephemeral_logs: Ephemeral link to logs (expires in <1 minute)
old_run_warning: Warning: the latest successful run is older than 90 days; artifacts may have expired.
preview_hint: If this workflow always produces exactly one artifact, you can drop '?preview' from the URL to redirect without naming the artifact.
status_param_hint: To allow any completed runs, not only successful ones, append ?status=completed to the URL.
adapt_url_hint: If you have several workflows or branches, you can adapt the URL by hand in a predictable way.
repo_not_found_private: Repository not found. If this is your private repository, authorize from the home page.
status_bad: Query parameter status must be 'success' (default) or 'completed'.
zip_only: Sorry, only *.zip downloads are supported for the moment.
artifact_expired: GitHub produced an error for this artifact download. Usually the artifact has expired (>90 days).
logs_expired: It seems that the logs for this job have expired.
analytics_lead_user: Statistics for artifact ZIP downloads in repositories you manage through the GitHub App.
analytics_lead_instance: Statistics for artifact ZIP downloads across all repositories connected to this dawnl.ink instance.
analytics_scope_instance: Instance owner view — all connected repositories
analytics_private_share_desc: Downloads that used a private ?h= token vs open links.
analytics_footnote: Client IPs are stored only as a salted hash. Raw ?h= tokens are never logged. Repository owners use GitHub OAuth (dashboard). The instance owner opens this page with DOWNLOAD_ANALYTICS_ADMIN_SECRET (?token= or X-Download-Analytics-Admin-Token).
